evals.prompt: Run e2e tests for this project.
evals.expected_output: Detects web app via apps/web/ + packages/playwright-web/, loads e2e-web.md reference, mentions storageState auth prerequisite, routes to turbo run e2e --filter=playwright-web
expectations: The response reads or references the e2e-detection.md reference file to determine app type
The response identifies the project as a web app (Next.js) based on apps/web/ and packages/playwright-web/
The response mentions storageState.json as a prerequisite or auth setup concern
The response includes or proposes the command turbo run e2e --filter=playwright-web
The response does NOT route to a marketing or desktop e2e strategy

evals.prompt: Run e2e tests. The tests ran and there were snapshot comparison failures in packages/playwright-marketing/.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT suggest auto-fixing snapshots. Escalates to user with explanation of how to manually update baselines using --update-snapshots. Shows playwright-report/ path for diff review.
expectations: The response explicitly states it will NOT auto-fix or auto-update the snapshots
The response mentions playwright-report/ as the location for the diff
The response provides the manual update command (npx playwright test --update-snapshots or equivalent)
The response explains the user should review the diff before updating baselines
The response does NOT run any snapshot update command automatically

evals.expected_output: Detects web app exists (apps/web/) but no packages/playwright-web/ config. Skips e2e gracefully. Offers to run /x4:e2e-setup to scaffold it.
expectations: The response detects that apps/web/ exists but packages/playwright-web/ does not
The response does NOT attempt to run e2e tests for the unconfigured web app
The response offers to run /e2e-setup or /x4:e2e-setup to scaffold e2e for the web app
The response gives the user options: scaffold now, skip, or continue without e2e
